nodes.label: Flint Dibble
nodes.summary: Mainstream archaeologist who debated Graham Hancock on the Joe Rogan Experience. Hancock argues Dibble's debating style revealed how academic archaeology deals with alternative viewpoints.

sources.quote: Mainstream archaeologist who debated Hancock on JRE

sources.timestamp_end: Hancock's new Netflix season with Keanu Reeves cameos

nodes.label: Ark of the Covenant (Axum tradition)
nodes.summary: Ethiopian tradition that the Ark of the Covenant rests in Axum (Tigray province); Hancock spent years investigating this while working as an East Africa correspondent.

sources.timestamp_end: Ethiopian tradition of Ark resting in Axum
Ethiopian city claimed as Ark of the Covenant resting place

nodes.label: Secret Societies & 'First Time' Transmission
nodes.summary: Hancock's framing: secret societies were entrusted with preserving knowledge from 'the First Time' — the antediluvian civilization — and passing it to future generations. The knowledge represents precocious astronomical/Milky-Way-journey knowledge not possessed today.

sources.timestamp_end: Hancock's frame of secret societies preserving first-civilization knowledge

nodes.label: Hancock argues his JRE debate with Flint Dibble revealed mainstream archaeology's 'win at all costs'
nodes.summary: Hancock argues his JRE debate with Flint Dibble revealed mainstream archaeology's 'win at all costs' approach to alternative viewpoints — Dibble repeatedly claims to have 'destroyed Graham' despite Hancock not feeling destroyed.

sources.quote: That debate has helped to show the general public how archaeology functions when it deals with people with alternative points of view. That function seems to be to win at all costs.

type_specific.statement_text: Hancock argues his JRE debate with Flint Dibble revealed mainstream archaeology's 'win at all costs' approach to alternative viewpoints — Dibble repeatedly claims to have 'destroyed Graham' despite Hancock not feeling destroyed.

nodes.label: Hancock posits that antediluvian secret societies preserve astronomical knowledge of 'the First Time
nodes.summary: Hancock posits that antediluvian secret societies preserve astronomical knowledge of 'the First Time' — including Milky-Way-journey cosmology via what is paradoxically called the 'underworld' in Egyptian tradition.

sources.quote: Secret societies which were entrusted with preserving information from what they call the first time and passing it down to future generations that represents such a precocious advanced astronomical knowledge — cosmic scale knowledge that we just don't have today.

type_specific.statement_text: Hancock posits that antediluvian secret societies preserve astronomical knowledge of 'the First Time' — including Milky-Way-journey cosmology via what is paradoxically called the 'underworld' in Egyptian tradition.

nodes.label: Hancock: Ethiopia is the only country in the world that claims to have the Ark of the Covenant — spe
nodes.summary: Hancock: Ethiopia is the only country in the world that claims to have the Ark of the Covenant — specifically at Axum in Tigray province; Hancock 'ate it, slept it, breathed it' for years as his first deep ancient-history investigation.

sources.quote: Ethiopia is the only country in the world that claims to have the Ark of the Covenant. The town is Axum in the province of Tigray in Northern Ethiopia. I ate it, I slept it, I breathed it, I dreamed it all the time.

type_specific.statement_text: Hancock: Ethiopia is the only country in the world that claims to have the Ark of the Covenant — specifically at Axum in Tigray province; Hancock 'ate it, slept it, breathed it' for years as his first deep ancient-history investigation.
